message:
fix(security): move Gemini API key from URL query param to request header

Previously the Gemini provider appended the API key as a ?key=... query
parameter in streaming_url().  This is problematic because:
- Query parameters appear in server access logs, proxy logs, and
  browser history
- CDNs and intermediate caches may log full URLs including secrets
- The Gemini API supports x-goog-api-key as a request header

Changes:
- streaming_url() no longer accepts an api_key parameter and no longer
  appends it to the URL
- The key is now sent via the x-goog-api-key header in the request
  builder within stream_response()
- All unit tests and backward-lock tests updated to verify the key is
  NOT in the URL and that the header-based auth path is used

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
